classObjective: Write equations of lines of fit in slope-intercept form using two points from a data set and use lines of fit to make predictions and evaluate the reliability of those predictions.
workedExamples: 5-3, Example 2 — Write and Use Lines of Fit (continued)
warmUp: What makes a line of fit 'good' for a scatter plot?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: choosing representative points from scatter plots; writing slope-intercept equations; interpolation vs. extrapolation caution
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing lines of fit from scatter plots and using them for predictions
independentPractice: Practice problems writing lines of fit and making/reliability-evaluating predictions
assessment: Exit ticket: write a line of fit from a scatter plot and use it to predict a value, noting limitations
capReflection: Why is extrapolation more risky than interpolation when using a line of fit?
